conclusion: The faster async-scheduling path remains unsafe for quality-preserving published results on this MiniMax/XPU graph configuration. Synchronous output copy does not fix the repeat drift, so the likely issue is upstream in async scheduling, XPU graph interaction, or decode input state ordering rather than only D2H output copy. Continue using the no-async quality-valid path while pursuing GPU-side fusion and collective-boundary reductions.
